prompt: Audit a bug-investigation conversation where the assistant read code (retry.ts:18), ran tests (pnpm test payments), saw user-reported prod metrics (5% during Black Friday), and then formed a hypothesis (thundering herd) and proposed a fix.

assertions.text: Uses the skill's three-level taxonomy (Facts/Verified/Observed/Stated; Inferences/Hypothesis/Pattern-based/Intent-inferred; Assumptions)
Classifies the retry.ts no-jitter observation under Facts/Verified and cites the file path (src/payments/retry.ts:18)
Classifies the pnpm test result under Facts/Observed with the command as evidence
Classifies the user's Black Friday 5% statement under Facts/Stated with a user quote
Classifies the thundering-herd / rate-limit diagnosis as an Inference (Hypothesis or Pattern-based), NOT as a Fact
Classifies the 'user wants fix before next weekend' claim as Intent-inferred (user never said this)
Demotes the assistant's hedge-worded claim 'Stripe usually throttles under concurrent bursts' to Inference/Pattern-based (the hedge word 'usually' signals inference)
Does NOT classify '4 callers, all in src/payments/' as a separate top-line fact — or if listed, classifies it under Facts/Observed (from the grep command) rather than Verified
Every Inference entry includes a concrete Verify action that names a specific command, file, or question (not 'check the logs' or 'investigate')
Every Assumption entry includes a concrete Verify action
Surfaces at least one genuine Assumption about the causal chain (e.g., that the failing /api/checkout calls actually pass through retry.ts, or that a3f21 deploy timing aligns with the 5xx cluster)
Does not invent evidence beyond the scenario (no fabricated log lines, stack traces, or file paths)
Omits empty sub-sections — does not emit a 'Verified' or other sub-header with no content under it
Does not include meta-claims about the audit itself (no 'the user is asking for an audit' type entries)
Stays under ~30 total bullet points across all sections (excluding section headers)
Does not duplicate the same claim across multiple sub-sections
